Class AlertManager

java.lang.Object
control.AlertManager
public class AlertManager extends Object
Monitors budget thresholds and sends notifications when spending milestones are reached.

Two thresholds are enforced:

  • 80 % – cycle status set to CycleStatus.NEAR_LIMIT and a warning notification is sent (once per cycle).
  • 100 % – cycle status set to CycleStatus.EXHAUSTED and a critical notification is sent.

  • Field Details

    • dbHelper

      private DatabaseHelper dbHelper
      Persistence helper used to read and update cycle status.

  • Constructor Details

    • AlertManager

      public AlertManager()
      Constructs an AlertManager backed by the singleton DatabaseHelper.

  • Method Details

    • sendNotification

      public void sendNotification(String message)
      Sends a notification message. Currently prints to stdout; intended to be replaced with a real notification mechanism (e.g., system tray alert).
      Parameters:
      message - the notification text to display

    • hasAlertBeenSent

      public boolean hasAlertBeenSent(int cycleId)
      Checks whether a threshold alert has already been sent for a cycle by inspecting its persisted status.
      Parameters:
      cycleId - the cycle ID to check (currently unused; reads from DB directly)
      Returns:
      true if the cycle status is CycleStatus.NEAR_LIMIT or CycleStatus.EXHAUSTED

    • checkThreshold

      public void checkThreshold(BudgetCycle cycle)
      Evaluates the cycle's spent percentage and fires the appropriate alert. The 80 % warning is only sent once per cycle; the 100 % alert fires every time spending reaches or exceeds the allowance.
      Parameters:
      cycle - the BudgetCycle to evaluate (must have totalSpent set)
